breakdown: This PR widens state flag checking to accept LLM numeric booleans (1, '1', 0, '0') alongside strict boolean values, fixing silent level-up stalls. The code uses careful type checking to avoid confusing bool subclasses with int values. Tests are comprehensive with good docstrings explaining the WHY behind the change.
key_changes: Widen _is_state_flag_true to accept int(1) and str('1') in addition to True/str('true')
Widen _is_state_flag_false to accept int(0) and str('0') in addition to False/str('false')
Add type check to ensure bool subclass of int is handled by bool branch, not int branch
Add comprehensive test class StateFlagSemanticsTest covering accepted and rejected values
Update docstrings to explain LLM numeric boolean rationale and production impact
